headline: Government pressure on Anthropic dominated the month as browser sandboxing techniques broke out alongside steady gains in inference memory engineering.
body: The clearest thread this window is escalating friction between the U.S. government and Anthropic: the Trump administration forced Anthropic to pull its cybersecurity models, a move framed around the Fable jailbreak but argued to be political rather than a genuine security failure, while Anthropic separately faces a lawsuit over Claude Max subscription limit claims. On the technical side, memory eviction broke out and KV cache quantization and system architecture all gained momentum, pointing to active work on inference memory engineering, while a cluster of new browser sandboxing entrants (sandbox attribute, Content Security Policy, iframe, srcdoc, origin isolation) appeared alongside RAG and cron jobs. Infrastructure strain also surfaced as Microsoft turned to AWS to address an AI capacity crunch at GitHub, and AWS WAF added a mechanism for content owners to charge AI bots for access. Tooling activity continued through Claude Code integrations, including a Visual Studio extension with native diff accept and reject.

cited_items.title: Microsoft turns to AWS as GitHub faces AI capacity crunch
Anthropic Sued over Alleged False Advertising on Claude Max Subscription Limits
The White House Is Ratcheting Up Its War Against Anthropic
Show HN: Claude Code for Visual Studio (native diff with accept/reject)
AWS WAF now lets content owners charge AI bots for access
